description: labor union
description_wiki: The International Longshore and Warehouse Union (ILWU) is a labor union which primarily represents dock workers on the West Coast of the United States, Hawaii, and in British Columbia, Canada; on the East Coast, the dominant union is the International Longshoremen's Association.

aliases: International Longshore and Warehouse Union Pensioners Association
description: US advocacy and social organization
city in and county seat of Snohomish County, Washington, United States of America
description_wiki: Everett ( EV-er-it; Lushootseed: dᶻəɬigʷəd [d͜zəˈɬi̞ɡʷəd]) is the county seat of and the most populous city in Snohomish County, Washington, United States.

description: city in and county seat of King County, State of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Seattle (  see-AT-əl) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Washington and the Pacific Northwest region of North America.

description: high school in Everett, Washington
description_wiki: Everett High School is a secondary school located in Everett, Washington, United States, which educates grades 9 through 12.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia between North Vietnam and South Vietnam, from 1955 to 1975
description_wiki: The Vietnam War (1 November 1955 – 30 April 1975) was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam (Democratic Republic of Vietnam) and South Vietnam (Republic of Vietnam) and their allies.

description: former US Army post in California
description_wiki: Fort Ord is a former United States Army post on Monterey Bay on the Pacific Ocean coast in California, which closed in 1994 due to Base Realignment and Closure (BRAC) action.

description: United States Army post in Kentucky, United States
description_wiki: Fort Knox is a United States Army installation in Kentucky, south of Louisville and north of Elizabethtown.

description: capital city of the United States of America
description_wiki: Washington, D.C., officially the District of Columbia and commonly known as simply Washington or D.C., is the capital city and federal district of the United States.

description: company
description_wiki: The Scott Paper Company was a manufacturer and marketer of sanitary tissue products with operations in 22 countries.

description: community college in Everett, Washington, USA
description_wiki: Everett Community College (EvCC) is a public community college in Everett, Washington.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Alaska (  ə-LASS-kə) is a non-contiguous U.S. state located in the northwestern regions of North America.

entity: Laborers' International Union of North America

description: peninsula
description_wiki: The Kenai Peninsula (Dena'ina: Yaghenen) is a large peninsula jutting from the coast of Southcentral Alaska.

description: census designated place in Matanuska-Susitna Borough, Alaska, United States
description_wiki: Big Lake (Dena'ina: K'enaka Bena)is a census-designated place (CDP) in Matanuska-Susitna Borough, Alaska, United States.

aliases: The City of Lights and Flowers
description: consolidated city-borough, and largest city in Alaska, United States of America
description_wiki: Anchorage (Denaʼina: Dgheyay Kaq'; Dgheyaytnu), officially the Municipality of Anchorage, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Alaska.

description: United States military facility in Anchorage, Alaska
description_wiki: Elmendorf Air Force Base (IATA: EDF, ICAO: PAED, FAA LID: EDF) is a United States Air Force (USAF) facility in Anchorage, Alaska.

description: bay in the Gulf of Alaska, USA
description_wiki: Cook Inlet (Denaʼina: Tikahtnu; Sugpiaq: Cungaaciq) stretches 180 miles (290 km) from the Gulf of Alaska to Anchorage in south-central Alaska.

description: city in Kenai Peninsula Borough, Alaska, United States
description_wiki: Homer (Dena'ina: Tuggeght) is a city in Kenai Peninsula Borough in the U.S. state of Alaska.

description: American timber company
description_wiki: The Weyerhaeuser Company ( WAIR-how-zər) is an American timberland company which owns approximately 10,400,000 acres (16,200 sq mi; 42,000 km2) of timberlands in the U.S., and manages an additional 14,000,000 acres (22,000 sq mi; 57,000 km2) of timberlands under long-term licenses in Canada.

description: November 5, 1916 armed confrontation between local vigilante "deputies" and members of the Industrial Workers of the World (IWW)
description_wiki: The Everett massacre, also known as Bloody Sunday, was an armed confrontation between local authorities and members of the Industrial Workers of the World (IWW) union, commonly called "Wobblies" in Everett, Washington, United States on November 5, 1916.

description: Labor dispute
description_wiki: On July 1, 1971, members of the International Longshore and Warehouse Union (ILWU) walked out against their employers, represented by the Pacific Maritime Association (PMA).

entity: American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations
